title: Multi-select questions
description: checkbox — the multi-select type. The answer is an array of chosen option values; fact_response_item fans it out to one row per selection, each carrying its own option_key. Count selections at this grain, never conflating them with respondent counts (fact_response keeps the respondent-question grain separate).

elements.name: languages
elements.title: Which languages do you use regularly?
elements.description: Validated exactly like a single-select: a non-empty, duplicate-free `choices` list. The difference is only downstream — an array answer like ["python", "sql"] becomes two fact rows, one per option_key.

elements.name: tools
elements.title: Which of these do you use?
elements.description: Object choices carry a stable machine `value` plus a human `text`, just like single-select; dim_option keys on `value`.
